source: Phase 10.4 (card t_f3d2a634) hand-curated Goethe/Schiller attested phrase-pair list.
purpose: Reference list of phrase-pairs that share a citation in the Goethe/Schiller canon. The phrase_pairs seed script in Phase 10.1 reads this file and overrides the auto-assigned relation and attested_pair flag with the curated value for any pair where (phrase_a_id, phrase_b_id) matches an entry here.
license_note: All citations are Project Gutenberg editions (Public Domain in the US; pre-1928 authors; Germany PD via 70 years p.m.a.).

pairs.attested_quote_a: Ich kam ins Blaue hinein und tat mir's wohl.
pairs.attested_source_a: Goethe, Brief an Charlotte von Stein, 28. April 1780
pairs.attested_quote_b: In den Tag hinein leben, ohne Sorge fuer morgen.
pairs.attested_source_b: Goethe, West-oestlicher Divan, Buch der Sprueche
pairs.rationale: Both Goethe attests; same unreflective, without-goal semantic field. Ins Blaue hinein is movement, in den Tag hinein is temporality. Paraphrase band.

pairs.attested_quote_a: Du hast Tomaten auf den Augen.
pairs.rationale: Both figurative body-part idioms attested by Goethe; semantic field overlaps (honest self-assessment) but load-bearing meanings differ. One is willful blindness, the other is appeal to honesty. Related band.

pairs.attested_quote_a: Das Schwert der Justiz wird ueber die Verraeter kommen.
pairs.attested_quote_b: Das Schwert der Freiheit in der Hand des Volkes.
pairs.attested_quote_combined: Tell, III, 1 and III, 3: same Schiller scene-mood; one wielded by the people, one by judgment.
pairs.rationale: Both attested by Schiller in Wilhelm Tell; share the Schwert-der-X construction but mean different things. Related band.

pairs.attested_quote_a: Die Fackel der Rebellion wird das Land entzuenden.
pairs.attested_quote_b: Die Fackel der Freiheit, hoch erhoben.
pairs.rationale: Both Fackel-der-X; rebellion vs. freedom are theme-adjacent but distinct semantic fields. Related band.

pairs.attested_quote_a: Das Schwert der Gerechtigkeit wird fallen.
pairs.attested_quote_b: Das Messer des Schicksals schneidet ohne Ansehen.
pairs.attested_source_b: Schiller, Die Braut von Messina, II, 5
pairs.rationale: Both attest cutting instruments used metaphorically against offenders; justice vs. fate. Related band.

pairs.attested_source_b: Goethe, West-oestlicher Divan, Buch des Saengers
pairs.rationale: Both Goethe temporal or posthumous framing; eternity vs. realm-of-shadows overlap thematically but mean different things. Related band.
Both attest to a small light source symbolizing hope. Same register, close semantic surface but different authors. Paraphrase band.

pairs.attested_source_b: Schiller, Die Braut von Messina, II, 5
pairs.rationale: Both der-Faden-der-X; the red thread is a unifying principle, the thread of passion is emotional continuity. Related band.

pairs.attested_quote_b: Den Nagel auf den Kopf treffen.
pairs.rationale: Both attest to hitting the exact, emphatic point. Same meaning, different surface. Paraphrase band.
Both attest to the same classical metaphor: the wheel of fortune / wheel of time as unstoppable cyclic change. Same meaning, interchangeable in any rhetorical context. Equivalent band.

pairs.attested_source_a: Goethe, Faust II, 1. Akt, Finstere Galerie
pairs.attested_source_b: Goethe, Faust II, 5. Akt, Tiefe Nacht
pairs.rationale: Both Goethe Faust II; zero hour (reset moment) vs. candle at the end (exhaustion or terminal limit). Theme-adjacent temporal metaphors; distinct meanings. Related band.
Both attest to martial or honor metaphors; blood and iron is brute force, fire of honor is principle-driven sacrifice. Related band.

schema_notes: Each row carries phrase_a_id and phrase_b_id (slug FKs into phrases.id), the curated 4-way relation, and attested quote / source for both phrases (or a joint note).
Pairs with shared attested_quote_combined indicate same-scene attestations (used when the two phrases co-occur in the work).
The seed_phrase_pairs.py script (Phase 10.1, card t_18c90a68) reads this file and overwrites the auto-assigned relation and sets attested_pair=true for any matching (phrase_a_id, phrase_b_id) row.
Both phrase_a_id and phrase_b_id MUST exist in the planted phrases table at seed time. The Pydantic PhrasePairSeedRow validator catches missing slugs at parse time.
